MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Robert H. Berkes of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant / moving party Calportland Company F/K/A California Portland Cement Company
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Carlos Gayton, Sr.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CALPORTLAND COMPANY F/K/A CALIFORNIA PORTLAND CEMENT COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant CalPortland Company F/K/A California Portland Cement Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant sustained its burden to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claim by demonstrating that plaintiff does not have an asbestos-related illness or injury. Dr. Bruch's report and declaration and Dr. Powers' report create a triable issue whether plaintiff has an asbestos-related illness or injury. As to the fear of cancer argument, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden on this issue. Even if defendant had sustained its burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Dr. Bruch's declaration create a triable issue whether plaintiff has a claim for fear of cancer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR # 8875 (503/TLJ)
